Submarine Acquisition / Torpedo Systems

       Lightweight Torpedo Depot Maintenance: Supports the repair, overhaul and refurbishment of LWT components and sub-assemblies at Navy organic and contractor
       activities. Includes repair of electronic circuit cards, Functional Item Replacements (FIR), and torpedo major sub sections including fuel tanks, warheads and nose arrays.
       Lightweight Torpedo Engineering/Logistics: Maintains an activity inventory of Lightweight Warshot and Exercise torpedoes, including the MK 46 Lightweight Torpedo
       (LWT) and MK54 and Vertical Launch Anti-Submarine Rockets (VLA) LWT through the FYDP. Provides materials, labor and testing required to turnaround LWTs
       which have been exercised to support Fleet firings or required Warshot maintenance due to expiration of maintenance cycle MK46 (12 years through FY 2015), MK54 (8
       years), and VLA (10 years). Provides the labor for LWT maintenance actions at NUWCDIVKPT. Provides ILS to meet LWT inventory and reliability objectives and Fleet
       load- out/tactical training requirements. Provides environmental control as well as analysis of weapon inventory. Provides SEA and Technical Direction Agent (TDA)
       functions, including reliability evaluation, failure analysis, logistics support, safety, environmental engineering, maintenance activity certification, and test equipment
       maintenance.

       MK48 Torpedo Ordnance Depot Maintenance: Supports the Repair, Overhaul and refurbishment of HWT components and sub-assemblies at Navy organic and
       contractor activities. Includes repair of electronic circuit cards, Functional Item Replacements (FIR), and torpedo major sub sections including fuel tanks, warheads and
       nose arrays.

       MK48 Torpedo Engineering/Logistics: Maintains an active inventory of Heavyweight (HWT) Warshot and Exercise Torpedoes. Provides material, labor and testing
       required to turnaround MK48 Advanced Capability (ADCAP), Mod 6, Mod 6 Advanced Common Torpedo (ACOT), and Mod 7 which have been exercised to support Fleet
       firings and for required Warshot maintenance due to expiration of maintenance cycle: MK 48 Mod 6 (6 years), and MK48 Mod 6 ACOT and Mod 7 (5 years). Prepares
       Depot Level Repairables (DLRs) for the HWT Intermediate Maintenance Facilities to support torpedo maintenance. Supports Naval Undersea Warfare Center, Keyport,
       WA (NUWCDIVKPT) government management and oversight of HWT Intermediate Maintenance Activity (IMA) at the Pearl Harbor facility. Provides progressive Depot
       Level Repair on MK48 HWT at Naval Submarine Torpedo Facility (NSTF) Yorktown, VA. Integrated Logistics Support (ILS) to meet HWT inventory and reliability
       objectives and Fleet load-out/tactical training requirements. Provides environmental control as well as analysis of weapon inventory. Provides Supportability Engineering
       Agent (SEA) and Technical Direction Agent (TDA) functions, including reliability evaluation, failure analysis, logistics support, safety, environmental engineering,
       maintenance activity certification, and test equipment maintenance.

Submarine Acquisition / Countermeasures

     Data Center Consolidation (DCC) Program: Implements the Navy Data Center and Commercial Cloud Resourcing Memorandum of Agreement and enables the use of
     either Government Cloud and/or Commercial Cloud services. In accordance with Department of Navy Chief Information Officer (DON CIO) established DCC policy
     guidance, this effort provides for the consolidation of inefficient infrastructure, optimization of existing facilities, improvement of security postures, achievement of cost
     savings, and transition to more efficient infrastructure, such as cloud services and inter-agency shared services.

     Sonar Towed Array Replenishment Spares: Systems supported include the TB-16 series, TB-23, TB-29 series, and the TB-34 series. This effort maintains an inventory
     of replenishment Intermediate Maintenance Activity (IMA) Consumables and Expendables spares and Depot repair parts are required to support corrective maintenance
     performed at the Intermediate Level and module repairs performed at the Depot Level maintenance activities. Seven Intermediate Maintenance Activities (IMAs) are
     operated by Lockheed Martin. Depots are operated by the various Towed Array Original Equipment Manufacturers (OEMs) and by the Government Owned/Contractor
     Operated (GO/CO) Depot located at the Naval Array Technical Support Center (NATSC) in Newport, RI. Maintenance includes evaluation and repair of Array modules
     which are damaged and modules that fail operational or calibration testing.

     SSTD Depot Level Repairable (DLR) Depot Maintenance: Surface Ship Torpedo Defense (SSTD) Program includes Countermeasure Anti Torpedoes (CAT), the
     Torpedo Warning System (TWS), AN/SLQ-25 (NIXIE) systems and the Acoustic Device Countermeasure (ADC) for surface ships. SSTD (DLR) Depot Maintenance
     supports dockside and at-sea certifications, Inventory Control Point (ICP) support, basic maintenance and program support. Basic maintenance includes all efforts
     associated with the repair and upkeep of all ATTDS and AN/SLQ-25NIXIE Systems and related Engineering Change Proposals (ECP) components. The AN/SLQ-25
     NIXIE and Torpedo Warning System (TWS) corrective maintenance is performed at the In-Service Engineering Activity (ISEA) located in Norfolk, VA. The CAT
     corrective maintenance is performed at the ISEA located in Keyport, WA.

     Surface Ship Torpedo Defense Replenishment Spares: Supports the procurement of Replenishment Spares for the AN/SLQ-25 system and Anti Torpedo Torpedo
     Defense System (ATTDS).

     Submarine Acoustic Warfare System (SAWS): Systems supported include externally mounted submarine countermeasures, internally launched countermeasures,
     acoustic augmentation, associated equipment, and program support. The externally launched system is made up of the Countermeasure Set Acoustic (CSA) MK 2 Series
     MODs, the second-generation CSA MK 3/4/5 Series System, MK 77 MOD 0/1 gas generators and Acoustic Device Countermeasures (ADC MK 3 MOD 1 and ADC MK
     4 MOD 1). Internally launched countermeasures include ADC MK 2, Naval Acoustic Electromechanical (NAE) Beacons and the Next Generation Countermeasure (ADC
     MK5).

     Submarine Acoustic Warfare Systems Replenishment Spares: Supports the inventory of replenishment spares and repair parts required for both In-Service Engineering
     Agent (ISEA) to support corrective and preventative maintenance performed at the Intermediate Level and launch assembly failure investigation and corrective repairs
     performed at the Depot Level maintenance activities.

Surface Undersea Warfare (USW)

        Carrier ASW Module Maintenance: Aircraft Carrier Tactical Support Center (CV-TSC) Maintenance provides Anti-Submarine Warfare (ASW)/Surface Warfare (SUW)
        coordination functions, to include the generation of real-time ASW/SUW information and recommendations, the detailed tactical planning and direction of ASW/SUW
        aircraft employment, performance of sensor data analysis, and distribution of tactically significant data to provide increased situational awareness to the Tactical Action
        Officer (TAO) and the Sea Combat Commander (SCC). Systems consist of commercial off the shelf (COTS) Navy Technical Insertion (TI) Common Display System
        (CDS) consoles, Common Processing System (CPS) enclosures, and Interface Processing System (IPS) enclosures.

        Forcenet - USW DSS: Undersea Warfare - Decision Support System (USW-DSS) provides an integrated, near real-time, net-centric Anti-Submarine Warfare (ASW)
        Command and Control (C2) capability. The system provides the Fleet with the capability to collaboratively plan and control USW operations across the battlespace. USW-
        DSS optimizes the alignment of sensors and monitors system performance to provide improved situational awareness. This enhances risk and vulnerability assessment,
        optimization of operations, and allocation of resources, all of which improve the lethality and survivability of the Fleet.

        Over the Horizon Weapon System (OTH WS) Sustainment: Provides for safe, reliable and mission capable operation of the Over the Horizon–Missile Launching
        System (OTH– MLS) program which is a non–developmental Acquisition Category (ACAT) II program capable of engaging a variety of surface threats at short and
        extended ranges. Efforts include recertification assessment/inventory assessments, data collection and analysis, analysis of Pre-flight and Telemetry Data, and component
        testing to identify changes that might affect the safety, reliability, and performance of the missile. Weapons system support; Integrated Logistics Support (ILS)
        Management, engineering/technical support, maintenance planning and maintenance, software support, including Information Assurance, technical data, configuration
        management, safety, packaging/handling/storage and transportation (PHS&T), and administrative efforts. The OTH–MLS will be installed and employed on multiple
        variants of the Littoral Combat Ship (LCS) and Guided Missile Frigate ship class (FFG). In addition, the OTH–MLS which consists of a Fire Control Suite / (FCS),
        Launching Mechanism / (LM), Encanistered Missile / (EM), will be considered a core system on three ship classes.

        Surface USW Depot: Overhauls and refurbishes deployed AN/WQC-2A and AN/WQC-6 Sonar Communication Sets, AN/UQN-4A and AN/UQN-10 Sonar Sounding
        Sets, AN/SRQ-4 Radio Sets, transducers and associated cables, and AN/SQQ-89A(V)15 Commercial-Off-the-Shelf (COTS) equipment. Includes facilities maintenance
        and equipment necessary to conduct overhauls and provide ISEA support; repair and refurbish AN/SQR-19 Towed Array Modules, and TB-37 Multi-function Towed
        Array (MFTA) modules.

        Surface USW Maintenance: Provides core Fleet support consisting of In-Service Engineering Agent (ISEA) support, Fleet logistics support/problem resolution, safety,
        maintenance and operational technical resolution, distant and on-site support for in-service Fleet (INSURV), casualty reports (CASREP), and technical assistance ship
        visits worldwide.

Standard Missile

        STANDARD Missile Weapons Maintenance (SM-2/SM-6) Non-Depot (NS): Provides Weapon System Support and Ordnance Assessments conducted by ten (10) field
        activities. Efforts include System Safety; Flight Analysis; Fleet Interface and Load outs; Missile Simulation; Missile Test and Evaluation Support; In-Service Battery

       Issues; Reliability; Packaging, Handling, Storage and Transportation; Business System Applications; Integrated Logistics Support; Depot Level Maintenance Facility
       management; and Dual Thrust Rocket Motor Rework STANDARD Missile Maintenance.

       STANDARD Missile Weapons SM-2/6 Maintenance and Depot Spares: Provides re-certification efforts, intermediate and depot level maintenance for the missile
       airframe, electronics, and all ordnance; Repairs required to correct In-Service issues, functional failures and deterioration of sub-assemblies required for asset readiness;
       Test Equipment maintenance; and Energetics and PHS&T maintained at Naval facilities.

       VLS Maintenance: MK 41 Vertical Launching System (VLS) supports operations and maintenance for the launcher and associated equipment/hardware onboard VLS
       equipped Cruisers (CGs) and Guided Missile Destroyers (DDGs). Support includes engineering and problem investigation, intermediate and depot level repair of canisters
       and launcher ancillary equipment, computer replacement tapes and industrial availability support to the VLS equipped Fleet. The MK 57 VLS is a modular, peripheral
       configuration missile launching system designed for DDG 1000 to provide missile launching capability for anti-air, anti-submarine, and strike warfare areas.

NATO Seasparrow (RIM-7)

       Depot Maintenance: Supports the Evolved SEASPARROW missile and MK 57 Mods 4-16. Depot level maintenance for missiles (ESSM) consists of failed missile
       section repairs from Fleet returns All Up Rounds (AURs) to re-issue missiles to the Fleet. Depot work for missiles is conducted through Raytheon Missiles and Defense
       Depot facility, with further distribution of repairs to international subcontractors in accordance with NATO Seasparrow Cooperative MOU arrangements.

       Engineering/logistics: Supports Evolved SEASPARROW missile and MK 57 NSSMS Mods 4-13. NATO SEASPARROW Surface Missile System MK 57 Mods 4-13
       (NSSMS) is a quick reaction weapon system with a mission of engaging self- defense threats utilizing its capability to search, detect, acquire, track and engage targets
       automatically or manually from search through intercept.

Rolling Air Frame (RAM) Maintenance

       Depot Maintenance: Depot maintenance includes the repair, assembly overhaul, refurbishment, rebuilding, software maintenance and testing of RAM missiles and
       launchers that require shop facilities, tooling, support equipment, personnel of higher technical skills, and processes beyond the organizational repair level capability.

       Non-Depot Maintenance: Provides technical support and assistance to the fleet including distance support and on-site technical assistance. It provides updates to
       Integrated Logistics Support (ILS) documentation including technical manuals, training documentation and ILS certifications; monitors reliability, maintainability, and
       supportability drivers; and supports investigations and development of solutions to issues affecting reliability, maintenance, logistics, and safety.

Close-In Weapon System (CIWS)

       CIWS MHA Re-baseline: The Phalanx Close-In Weapons System (CIWS) is a fast reaction, rapid fire, computer-controlled radar and 20mm gun designed to engage Anti-
       Ship Missiles (ASM). The Block 1B Surface Mode Upgrade adds a capability to counter small, high speed surface threats and low-slow air threats.

        Depot Maintenance: Depot maintenance includes the repair, assembly overhaul, refurbishment, rebuilding, software maintenance and testing of CIWS mounts that require
        shop facilities, tooling, support equipment, personnel of higher technical skills, and processes beyond the organizational repair level capability.

        Systems and Maintenance Engineering: Provides technical support and assistance to the fleet including distance support and on-site technical assistance through updates
        to Integrated Logistics Support (ILS) documentation including technical manuals, training documentation and ILS certifications; monitors reliability, maintainability, and
        supportability drivers; and supports investigations and development of solutions to issues affecting reliability, maintenance, logistics, and safety.

Stalker: Support includes the In-Service Engineering Agent (ISEA), repair and support efforts required for the fielded Stalker system, and Product Support activities, including
analyzing the Integrated Logistics Support (ILS) requirements, timeline, Configuration Management, Supply Support Planning, Maintenance Planning and Provisioning, Technical
Data, Installation Documentation, Training and ILS Certification.

Joint Advanced Strike Technology / Gun Weapons System Replacement Program (GWSRP: Supports MK38 Machine Gun System (MGS), MK46 Gun Weapon System (GWS), and
MK34 GWS pier side maintenance efforts; standardized pier side maintenance and repairs (SPMRs) on MK45 gun mounts, MK86 Gun Fire Control Systems (GFCS), MK160 gun
computer system (GCS), MK46 Optical Sight System (OSS), and MK20 Electro-Optical Sight System (EOSS); Major, Medium, and Minor Caliber Gun 2J COG Equipment
overhauls; Major, Medium, and Minor Caliber Gun In-service engineering/logistics support; Naval Fires Control System (NFCS) logistics, maintenance, in-service engineering,
Naval Surface Fire Support (NSFS) certification, and NSFS capabilities transition to Fires Planner support; and Major and Minor caliber Gun, MK86 Gun Fire Control System
(GFCS), MK160 Gun Computer System (GCS), MK46 Optical Sight System (OSS), and MK20 Electro-Optical Sight System (EOSS) Fleet Support.

2T Ammunition: The 2T Ammunition program provides inventory management, technical assessment and maintenance functions for NAVSEA (2T Cog) ammunition. This
includes: requirements planning, and configuration/control throughout the ammunition life cycle; equipment/system maintenance and updates; Quality Assurance (QA)/statistical
process control support; Ordnance Information System (OIS) interface; implementation of acquisition reform; ammo malfunction investigations; preparation of depot maintenance
work requirements; performing depot maintenance; update technical manuals; performing evaluation and maintenance of overseas ammo stock with the use of Mobile Ammunition
Evaluation and Reconditioning Unit (MAERU).


Littoral & Mine Warfare (LMW) / Small Arms Support

        In-Service Engineering: NSWC provides In-Service engineering for all Navy managed assets. These duties include all design, development, testing, in-service repairs, and
        disposal of Navy Small Arms. In-Service engineering is responsible for providing management, engineering and logistic support functions for the weapons and mounts.
        Assignments include Technical Direction Agent (TDA), Systems Integration Agent (SIA), Design Agent (DA), Acquisition Engineering Agent (AEA), and Production
        Engineering Agent (PEA). These include providing management and engineering and logistic support functions for the weapons and mounts, .50cal and below.

        Small Arm Distribution: Small Arms Distribution funds are provided to the Supply warehouse to track shipping records and current shipping traffic of all small arms.

        Small Arms Repair: Intermediate maintenance of Naval Special Warfare and Navy's common small arms weapons, 40MM Grenade Launcher, .50 caliber and below.
        Weapons repair as well as modernization and procurement of new navy gun mounts are performed in this budget activity. The weapons repaired become available as ready



                                                                                                                                                                Exhibit OP-5, 1D4D

                                                                     FY 2026 President's Budget Submission
                                                                        Operation and Maintenance, Navy

                                                                         Activity Group: Weapons Support
                                                                Detail by Subactivity Group: Weapons Maintenance

       for issue assets to be issued to activities worldwide as well as Naval Special Warfare (SEAL teams) with small arms allowances as part of the small arms life cycle
       management program. Centralized life cycle management of Small Arms Weapons and mounts as well as Security Equipment for approximately 2,000 activity allowances,
       include Navy, US Marine Corps Aviation, US Coast Guard and Military Sealift Command.
       Small Arms Tracking: The Gun Control Act of 1976 and DoD 4140.1 require the services to establish, control, and fund the automated registration and tracking of all
       small arms in the inventory.

       Expeditionary Loitering Munitions Air System: The Navy is pursuing a commercial-off-the-shelf solution with autonomous government-provided software and a
       government-provided launcher system. The system will be expeditionary and deployable by small teams to support operations in various environments. The system
       architecture shall be modular and extensible to allow for capability upgrades and periodic technology refresh.

       Expeditionary Loitering Munitions Surface System: The Navy is pursuing a commercial-off-the-shelf solution with autonomous government-provided software . The
       system will be expeditionary and deployable by small teams to support operations in various environments. The system architecture shall be modular and extensible to allow
       for capability upgrades and periodic technology refresh.

Littoral & Mine Warfare (LMW) / Mines and Mine Countermeasures (MCM)

       AN/AQS-20A Sonar System: The AN/AQS-20 is a mine hunting and identification system with sensors housed in an underwater towed body. The sensors are designed for
       the detection, classification and localization of bottom, close-tethered, and volume targets, and also for the identification of bottom targets. The system will be deployed
       from the Littoral Combat Ship (LCS) as part of the MCM Mission Package or can be deployed from other Vessels of Opportunity (VOO).

       ASSAULT BREACHING SYSTEM (ABS): The Shallow Water MCM funding provides maintenance support for the Breach Lane Navigation (BLN) systems and the
       Coastal Battlefield Reconnaissance Analysis (COBRA) for the Littoral Combat Ships (LCS) Mission Module.

       Knifefish (SMCM UUV): The Knifefish Surface Mine Countermeasure Unmanned Undersea Vehicle (SMCM UUV) program procures Unmanned Undersea Vehicles to
       support clandestine mine detection capability against volume and bottom mines including buried mine detection. The Knifefish system is composed of two UUVs,
       batteries, launch and handling system, and other support equipment. Maintenance of systems and components includes screening, maintenance, testing, adjustments,
       replacement, repair, modification and test equipment. In addition, this program provides sonar In-Service Engineering Agent (ISEA) for overall engineering, maintenance,
       installation and logistic support for sonar systems; and ISEA and Technical Design Agent (TDA) functions including overall system engineering fleet support, maintenance,
       repair, logistic support, Casualty Report (CASREP) coordination, and tech manual upgrades.

       LCS Mission Modules (LCS MM): The LCS Mission Modules Program includes the Mine Countermeasure Mission Package (MCM MP) and the Surface Warfare Mission
       Package (SUW MP). The program will provide Surface Warfare (SUW) Mission Module In-Service Engineering Agent (ISEA) for overall engineering, maintenance,
       installation, and logistic support for the mission module system and components and ISEA and Technical Design Agent (TDA) functions, including overall system
       engineering fleet support, maintenance, repair, logistic support, Casualty Reports (CASREPs) coordination, and tech manual upgrades.




                                                                                                                                                                Exhibit OP-5, 1D4D
        351                                                                                                                                                         (Page 11 of 33)
                                                                     Department of the Navy
                                                             FY 2026 President's Budget Submission
                                                                Operation and Maintenance, Navy

                                                                 Activity Group: Weapons Support
                                                        Detail by Subactivity Group: Weapons Maintenance

MCM Class Sonar/Combat Systems: Provides maintenance and depot level support of systems and components associated with all mine hunting sonars and combat
systems (navigation, neutralization, and sweeping) aboard active Mine Countermeasures (MCM) class ships. Maintenance of systems and components includes screening,
maintenance, testing, adjustments, replacement, repair, modification and test equipment. In addition, this program provides sonar In-Service Engineering Agent (ISEA) for
overall engineering, maintenance, installation and logistic support for sonar systems; and ISEA and Technical Design Agent (TDA) functions including overall system
engineering fleet support, maintenance, repair, logistic support, CASREP coordination, and tech manual upgrades for MCM and MHC class ships in the active fleet. Units
represent the number of ships with mine hunting sonars and combat systems equipment in the active fleet.

MCM Engineering/logistics: Provides engineering and maintenance support of the following service and Exercise and Training (E&T) mines: Mine Mk 56, 60, 62, 63, 65
and 67. This effort also provides for E&T mines MK 6,25,36,52, and 55 used for both mine and MCM training and Targets MK 44, 45, 46, 47, 48, 49, 50, 51, 52, and 53,
used for MCM training.

Mine Warfare Environmental Decision Aid Library (MEDAL): Provides support for Mine Warfare and Environmental Decision Aids Library (MEDAL), the single
approved Tactical Decision Aid for all Mine Warfare functionality within the US Navy command and control system. As such, MEDAL interfaces with all existing MIW
systems to provide a seamless flow of tactical information to and from MIW units and all warfare commanders. The system also provides the coordination necessary for
common situational awareness within the mine warfare community users and non-mine warfare equipped fleet users. It supports the expeditionary hardware for GCCS-M4.
X. It supports fielding of Course of Action Planner (COAP) for the Mine Warfare Commander. COAP provides automated algorithms to assist in mission planning for the
Mine Warfare Command.

Mine countermeasure Unmanned Surface Vehicle (MCMUSV): The Mine Countermeasures Unmanned Surface Vehicle (MCM USV) [previous term CUSV] with
Payloads program leverages the USV from the Unmanned Influence Sweep System (UISS) Program of Record and adds a modular mission capability through the addition
of multiple payloads. MCM USV w/AQS-20C integrates the existing AQS-20C mine-hunting sonar. Maintenance of systems and components includes screening,
maintenance, testing, adjustments, replacement, repair, modification and test equipment. In addition, this program provides vehicle and sonar In-Service Engineering
Agent (ISEA) for overall engineering, maintenance, installation and logistic support for the sonar Deploy and retrieve (D&R) systems; and ISEA and Technical Design
Agent (TDA) functions including overall system engineering fleet support, maintenance, repair, logistic support, Casualty Report (CASREP) coordination, and tech
manual upgrades. In addition, this program provides sweep In-Service Engineering Agent (ISEA) for overall engineering, maintenance, installation and logistic support for
the sweep payload systems; and ISEA and Technical Design Agent (TDA) functions including overall system engineering fleet support, maintenance, repair, logistic
support, CASREP coordination, and tech manual upgrades.

Mines Engineering Support: Provides engineering support for the following in-service mines: The QUICKSTRIKE (QS) family of air delivered mines - the MK 62, MK
63, MK 65, MK 65 (All Mods), SLMM MK 67, VEMS and MCM Targets.

Mines Maintenance: Provides maintenance and support for the following in-service mines: The QUICKSTRIKE (QS) family of air delivered mines the MK 62, MK 63,
MK 65, MK 65 (All Mods), SLMM MK 67, VEMS and MCM Targets.
Orca Extra Large Unmanned Undersea Vehicle (XLUUV): The Orca Extra Large Unmanned Undersea Vehicle (XLUUV) is the Navy's Extra Large UUV effort as part
of the UUV Family of Systems (FoS). The Orca XLUUV effort has been established to address a Joint Emergent Operational Need (JEON). Orca XLUUV will have a
modular payload bay, with defined interfaces that current and future payloads must adhere to for employment from the vehicle. This OMN funding is in support of In-Service


                                                                                                                                                      Exhibit OP-5, 1D4D
 352                                                                                                                                                      (Page 12 of 33)
                                                                                Department of the Navy
                                                                        FY 2026 President's Budget Submission
                                                                           Operation and Maintenance, Navy
                                                                           Budget Activity: Operating Forces
                                                                            Activity Group: Weapons Support
                                                                   Detail by Subactivity Group: Weapons Maintenance

         Engineering Agent (ISEA), UUV operation center, facilities support, forward operating base and Software Support Activity (SSA) efforts including engineering,
         maintenance and logistics support.

         RAZORBACK UUV: Littoral Battlespace Sensing – Autonomous Undersea Vehicle Submarine Variant (LBS-AUV(S)) – RAZORBACK within this sub-activity group
         supports the performance of Intelligence of the Operational Environment (IPOE) for submarine missions. Supports In-Service Engineering Agent (ISEA) and Software
         Support Activity (SSA) efforts including engineering and logistics support.

         Surface Mine Countermeasure (SMCM): Mine Countermeasures program provides maintenance of systems and components associated with all mine hunting sonars and
         combat systems (navigation, neutralization, and sweeping) aboard active Mine Countermeasures (MCM) class ships. In addition, this program provides sonar In-Service
         Engineering Agent (ISEA) for overall engineering, maintenance, installation and logistic support for sonar systems; and ISEA and Technical Design Agent (TDA)
         functions including overall system engineering fleet support, maintenance, repair, logistic support, CASREP coordination, and tech manual upgrades for MCM class ships
         in the active fleet. The combat systems are supported by Conditional Assessment and Repair Efforts (CARE) and Special MNS Assessment and training Team (SMARTT)
         efforts. Mine warfare tactics is also developed along with obsolescence studies. Units represent the number of ships with mine hunting sonars and combat systems
         equipment in the active fleet.

         Small Unmanned Surface Vehicle (sUSV): sUSV is a family of systems designed to meet an urgent Geographic Combatant Command Requirement. The sUSV platform
         will be delivered with autonomy ready package with remote command and control capabilities capable of carrying various payloads. This program provides training,
         maintenance, and sustainment to sUSV Family of Systems consisting of sUSV, Global Autonomous Reconnaissance Craft (GARC), and sUSV Production-Ready,
         Inexpensive, Maritime Expeditionary (PRIME). The program will provide sUSV Family of Systems In-Service Engineering Agent (ISEA) support, storage, training
         classes, as well as software sustainment including licenses, C2 station, and Software Support Activity (SSA).

Surface Training Advanced Virtual Environment (STAVE): Surface Training Advanced Virtual Environment (STAVE) provides training to warfare centers that rapidly
qualifies Sailors in instructional and simulated physical environments that support the MCM mission.

Surveillance Towed Array Sensor System (SURTASS) Depot Spares: The mission of Maritime Surveillance Systems includes detection, classification, localization, tracking, and
reporting of nuclear and diesel submarines in Deep Ocean and littoral regions. The Surveillance Towed Array Sensor System (SURTASS) provides mobile surveillance capability
using T-AGOS class ships. It is the tactical and strategic arm of the Navy's undersea surveillance capability which provides deep ocean and littoral acoustic detection and cueing for
tactical weapons platforms against diesel and nuclear submarines in any given Area of Operations worldwide.

Tetra: Tetra is a remotely operated vehicle (ROV) with the capacity for multiple mission payloads as key enabler to Subsea and Seabed Warfare (SSW). Tetra is payload for SSNs and
SSGNs by expanding current capabilities not offered by either submarines or unmanned undersea vehicles (UUV) through real time situational awareness and SSW effects.

BootStrap: BootStrap develops payloads/packages to meet the evolving threat by leveraging ongoing Navy, Navy partner, and commercial research. Efforts including processing and
command and control technology.

Classified Programs: Supports classified efforts, whose details are held at a higher classification and are submitted to Congress via separate channels.

II. Force Structure Summary:
The Air-Launched Missile Rework Program: Provides depot maintenance, operational assessments, and logistics support of 15 weapon systems in support of all Type Model
Series (T/M/S) aircraft resulting in support to 213 fleet squadrons.

The Air-Launched Ordnance Rework Program: Provides depot maintenance, operational assessments, and logistics support of 15 weapon systems in support of all T/M/S aircraft
resulting in support to 272 fleet squadrons.

 The Navy Mission Planning System Program: Provides software & hardware maintenance, user training, and help desk support for 43 T/M/S and over 4000 Joint Mission
 Planning Systems laptop computers & servers.

 The GUNSS (Geo-Spatial Intelligence Unified Naval Streaming System) Program: Supports fifty-one (51) CVN, LHA/LHD, CG, LPD, LCC, LSD, DDG Navy ships and
 Tactical Operations Center sites, with plans to increase to ninety (90) by the end of FY 2025. The program has moved from an individual licenses, per ship, to an enterprise license
 to allow unlimited installations as required.

The Weapons Engineering Technical Services Program: Provides 46 work years of on-site, direct fleet support subject matter expertise of all PEO U/W managed weapons
programs and aircraft interface system troubleshooting, training. Provides on-site Organizational, Intermediate, and program office directed Depot maintenance repairs. The
Weapons Technical Services program employees are embedded at 23 key major Navy and Marine Corps military installations (CONUS and OCONUS).

INDOPACOM-Special Operation Command (SOCPAC) Intelligence, Surveillance and Reconnaissance (ISR) Program in the Philippines for Regional Operations and
support/integration services:

Flight hours - 309 hours/month with two COCO Manned Platforms: King Air 350 and CL605 Monthly
Fuel: 351,500


   AFRICOM Reconnaissance Intelligence Exploitation Services (ARIES): Provides enterprise Intelligence, Service, and Reconnaissance (ISR) and ancillary services to
   USAFRICOM and Commander Naval Forces Europe-Africa/Commander 6th Fleet (CNE-CNA-C6F) (NAVEUR-NAVAF) in order to achieve DoD outcomes.

   The Collaborative Mission Planning Continuum (CMPC) Program (formerly The Next Generation Naval Mission Planning System (NGNMPS) Program): Provides
   software and hardware maintenance, user training, repair, and sustainment of Fleet Released Naval Open Mission Systems and CPMC ashore and afloat for 5-6 T/M/S aircraft
   in FY25, with systems for additional T/M/S aircraft to be fielded overtime, eventually totaling 28 T/M/S.

 Air Wing Ship Integration Program: Provides limited software & hardware maintenance and user training for eight (8) NIMITZ-Class (CVN-68) ships.

 The Unmanned Aircraft Systems (UAS) supported include the following 11 programs:



                                                                                                                                                                  Exhibit OP-5, 1D4D
          354                                                                                                                                                         (Page 14 of 33)
                                                                         Department of the Navy
                                                                 FY 2026 President's Budget Submission
                                                                    Operation and Maintenance, Navy
                                                                    Budget Activity: Operating Forces
                                                                    Activity Group: Weapons Support
                                                           Detail by Subactivity Group: Weapons Maintenance
      a.   The Fire Scout MQ-8 Program: The program procured a total of 68 air vehicles thirty (30) MQ-8Bs and thirty-eight (38) MQ-8Cs. The program sustains
           Nineteen (19) MQ- 8C Fire Scouts with no further contract deliveries. Fifteen (15) MQ-8Cs are located at the Fleet Contractor Field Team (CFT) supported
           storage and maintenance facility at Naval Base Ventura County (NBVC) Pt. Mugu, CA. Two (2) MQ-8C Fire Scouts are at Webster Field, MD. Two (2) MQ-
           8Cs were stricken due to attrition. All MQ-8B's have been fully sun downed as of October 2022. Navy decision to fully divest MQ-8C will result in FY25
           transfer of all Thirty-Six (36) Air Vehicles located at Naval Air Station (NAS) North Island, CA, Naval Base Ventura County (NBVC) Pt. Mugu, CA and
           Webster Field, MD to the Aerospace Maintenance and Regeneration Group (AMARG) facility for storage and ultimate reuse or disposal.

      b.   The MQ-4C Triton Program: The program will procure twenty-five (25) fleet Air Vehicles (AVs) and associated Mission Control Stations (MCS) and
           Forward Operating Bases (FOB) to support three (3) OCONUS operational orbits, one (1) test site, and two (2) homeport locations. The OCONUS inventory
           will be comprised of four (4) AVs and one (1) FOB per orbit. In FY25, the Triton program will be sustaining twelve (12) AVs, ten (10) MCSs and five (5)
           FOBs supporting all three (3) operational orbits, one (1) test site, one (1) homeport location and initial stand-up of the 2nd homeport location.

      c.   The Reaper MQ-9 Program: Provides the USMC with the full sustainment of twenty (20) air vehicles (AV), sixteen (16) Ground Control Stations (GCS),
           and all required ancillary equipment to complete the MQ-9A UAS. In FY20, two (2) AV and two (2) GCSs were procured to support operations in the
           USCENTCOM area of responsibility. In FY23, the USMC will accept delivery of six (6) AV and two (2) GCSs to support operations in USINDOPACOM
           and USNORTHCOM. In FY24, the USMC will accept delivery of twelve (12) AV and four (4) GCSs to support operations in USCENTCOM,
           USINDOPACOM, USNORTHCOM and the establishment of the first USMC MQ-9A OPTEVFOR squadron. In FY25, the USMC will accept delivery of two
           (2) AV to support MQ-9A training and test squadrons, and eight (8) GCSs to support operations in USCENTCOM, USINDOPACOM, and USNORTHCOM.
           Operations and maintenance will rely on contracted logistics support for maintenance, air crew, and supply until organic capability is established by the
           USMC. All organizational, intermediate, and depot level maintenance will be contracted until the USMC begins the transition to organic maintenance stand-
           up in FY26.

      d.   The Navy Family of Small UAS (FoSUAS) Program (formerly PUMA (RQ-20): Provides approximately two hundred seventy-six (276) Nano UAS, two
           hundred thirty (230) of which are fielded to NSW and forty-six (46) to the NECF. Provides approximately four hundred thirty (430) Micro UAS, two hundred
           twenty (220) of which are fielded to NSW and two hundred ten (210) to the NECF. Provides approximately one hundred thirty-one (131) SR/SE UAS
           systems, ninety-four (94) of which are fielded to NSW and thirty-seven (37) to the NECF. Provides approximately ninety-one (91) MR/ME UAS systems,
           ninety (90) of which are fielded to NSW and one (1) to the NECF.

      e.   The ARCANE (ARchitecture and Capabilities for Autonomy in the Naval Enterprise) Program: Force Structure is Not Applicable.

      f.   The Blackjack RQ-21 Program: Provides ten (10) in full operational use by Naval Special Warfare (NSW), and twenty-one (21) thathave been stricken from
           USMC fleet inventory due to divestment. Of the 21 stricken USMC UA, seventeen (17) are in the Reclamation and Disposal Program (SARDIP) for parts use
           at NIWC ATL in Charleston SC and SOFSA, Special Operations Forces Support Activity, in Lexington KY. One system is in use by UX-24 at Webster
           Outlying Field in St. Inigoes MD as a test asset.

              g.   The MQ-25 Stingray Program: Provides forty-six (46) Primary Aircraft Authorization (PAA) air vehicles (AV) and all required ancillary equipment for two
                   (2) MQ-25 UAS operational squadrons. MQ-25 squadrons are comprised of four (4) CONUS detachments on each coast and one (1) Forward Deployed Naval
                   Force (FDNF) Detachment for a total of nine (9) detachments. Post IOT&E in 4QFY26, five (5) EMD AV will be productionized for initial Fleet Operations
                   with the first MQ-25 squadron, VUQ-11 Detachment 1, based at NBVC Pt Mugu along with the Fleet Replenishment Squadron (FRS) VUQ-10. Initial CVN
                   deployment on the USS CARL VINSON CVN-70 will commence in FY27. Additional System Developmental Test Asset (SDTA), Low-Rate Initial
                   Production (LRIP), and Full Rate Production (FRP) AV will start delivery in FY28. AVs will be assigned to the first East Coast MQ-25 Squadron, VUQ-12
                   Detachment 1, based at NS Norfolk, in FY29. VUQ-11 FDNF Detachment based at MCAS Iwakuni Japan (Pre-decisional) will stand up in FY31. Final
                   detachment standup is scheduled for FY35 and procurement of all seventy-six (76) Total Active Inventory (TAI) AV is scheduled to be completed in FY38.

              h.   The Unmanned Carrier Aviation (UCA) Mission Control Station (UMCS) Program: Provides fourteen (14) Operational MD-5 Unmanned Carrier
                   Aviation Mission Control System (UMCS) with associated software & hardware maintenance, user training, repair, and sustainment for two (2) CVNs being
                   installed in FY25, including the USS CARL VINSON CVN-70 supporting initial operational deployment for MQ-25 in FY26. The first ashore is scheduled at
                   NBVC Pt Mugu in in FY27. Systems for additional MD-5 control stations will continue being fielded annually, eventually totaling ten (11) CVNs and three (3)
                   ashore locations at NBVC Pt Mugu, NS Norfolk, and MCAS Iwakuni Japan (Pre-decisional).

              i.   The Scan Eagle-ISR Services OIR Program: Provides 4,500 hours of imagery per month at numerous forward operating bases throughout the battlespace
                   (JUONS CC-0549).

              j.   The Scan Eagle-ISR Services Sea Program: Provides 1,000 sensor hours per month aboard five (5) U.S. Ships (CENTCOM RFF 1329).

              k.   The Scan Eagle-ISR Services DDG Fleet Program (formerly the Scan Eagle-ISR Services 7th Fleet program): Provides Tactical UAS support aboard
                   three (3) U.S. Ships with plans to increase to six (6) U.S. Ships (Top Level Requirement).

 The Software Sustainment Support Program (formerly Program Related Engineering): Provides support for 12 weapon system platforms in support of all Type Model
 Series (T/M/S) aircraft resulting in support to 213 fleet squadrons.

 The Sustainment Logistics Support Program (formerly Program Related Logistics): Force Structure is Not Applicable.

 The Tomahawk Weapons System (TWS) Program: Provides support for 139 total firing units (FRUs) comprised of 85 Surface and 54 Subsurface (including 4 SSGNs).

The Theater Mission Planning Center (TMPC) Program: Provides four scalable configurations of TMPC deployed at 183 sites. The sites are made up of 139 FRU's (85
Surface and 54 Subsurface), 4 Cruise Missile Support Activities (3 US and 1 FMS), 3 Tomahawk Strike Mission Planning Cells (one in each Commander, 5th, 6th and 7th Fleet),
21 Carrier Strike Groups (11 CVN, and 10 Shore Sites), and 16 Fleet Training Sites and Labs.

 MK48 Heavyweight Torpedo Ordnance Depot Maintenance Program: Support for Heavyweight Torpedo Maintenance is located at the Naval Submarine Torpedo Facility.
 In addition, it involves 309 flight hours per month with two (2) COCO Manned Platforms - King Air 350 and CL605. Monthly Fuel is 351,500

I. Description of Operations Financed:
Programs funded in the Enterprise Information Technology budget line item provide foundational support for the Department of the Navy (DoN) Information Superiority Vision.
The DoN must securely deliver the right information to the right Sailor or Marine at the right time to defeat high-paced and evolving threats. As we adopt a model that allows us to
modernize, innovate, and defend our platform, we unlock this future. The network itself becomes a warfighting platform.

This budget continues to support key focus areas of Naval Cloud Productivity and Data Availability. The DoN continues to shift enterprise collaboration and productivity tools
and services to a cloud-based Microsoft 365 solution with improved security. The DoN works to provide access to trustworthy and understandable data that supports mission-
centered analytics

This budget also continues efforts to consolidate DoN financial systems and enhance cybersecurity controls to improve data integrity. A modern systems environment replaces
components and reduces a need for manual intervention. An agile disparate architecture supporting an efficient network of applications, services, suppliers, and capabilities will
allow DoN to innovate, pivot rapidly and automate business processes. Funding supports ongoing efforts to consolidate into two DoN General Ledgers and decommission legacy
feeder systems to streamline the DoN's systems environment and increase compliance with Federal Information Technology standards, including the Federal Information System
Controls Audit Manual and Federal Financial Management Improvement Act.

This subactivity groups critical funded Information Technology programs in the following categories:

Enterprise Network Architecture
Next Generation Enterprise Network (NGEN)
Navy Commercial Cloud Services (NCCS)
Oracle Enterprise Software
Navy Enterprise Service Desk (NESD)
Program Executive Office for Digital and Enterprise Services (PEO DES)
Data Center Consolidation and Navy Cloud Migration
Outside the continental United States (OCONUS) Navy Enterprise Network (ONE-Net) programs

Data Analytics and Management Decision Support Tools
Navy JUPITER
Force Level Integration (FLINT)
DoD IT Portfolio Repository (DITPR)-DoN
DoN Application and Database Management System (DADMS)
Navy Medical Information Technology
Visibility & Management of Operation & Support Costs (VAMOSC)
Information Technology Services (NAV-ITAS)

Financial Management and Defense Business Systems
Navy Enterprise Resource Planning (ERP)
        Accounting Systems:
                 Standard Accounting, Budgeting, and Reporting System (SABRS)

Enterprise Network Infrastructure

Next Generation Enterprise Network (NGEN): The Naval Enterprise Networks program mission is to provide an enterprise approach to delivery of a fully integrated,
interoperable, and secure networking platform. Navy Marine Corps Intranet (NMCI) and Outside the Continental United States (OCONUS) Navy Enterprise Network (ONE-Net)
provides secure, net-centric data and information technology (IT) capabilities and services to support United States Navy (USN) and United States Marine Corps (USMC)
warfighters and users. ONE-Net is a program of record belonging to Naval Information Warfare Systems Command that modernizes the Navy's antiquated OCONUS
infrastructure by installing state-of-art IT capability where no capability or legacy systems exist; currently at sixteen major OCONUS Fleet concentration bases and stations, ONE-
Net is based on NMCI architecture and delivers an NMCI equivalent capability. It serves as one of the three FORCEnet enterprise network pillars, and further satisfies increased
demand for Secure Internet Protocol Router Network (SIPRNet) at locations servicing forward deployed and support forces. The network is migrating toward commercial standard
approaches to improve end-user experience and application performance, enabling optimal consumption of cloud-based services, allowing for greater accessibility to critical data;
expanding the mobile platform; and shifting to a framework that emphasizes "data protection" as a fundamental element of cybersecurity. NGEN provides 522K Navy personnel at
approximately 500 sites around the globe with both on-premise and remote digital and enterprise services. These capabilities are essential for personnel to design, deliver, and
sustain Navy combat power. The NGEN program continuously modernizes solutions by leveraging "state-of-the-art" commercial technology insertion. In FY 2024, NGEN
performance categories changed to provide more fidelity and transparency in fixed and variable costs across the Navy.

                         NGEN
        Next Generation Enterprise Network Management
        Next Generation Enterprise Network Enterprise Software Management
        Next Generation Enterprise Network Cybersecurity
        Next Generation Enterprise Network Information Transport
        Next Generation Enterprise Network Non-IT Infrastructure
        Next Generation Enterprise Network Cloud Services
        USN Seats Supported CONUS & OCONUS

Commencing in FY26, NGEN will evolve its definitions of performance categories to optimize its portfolio management and agile operations around value streams, expressed in 4
capability-based goals:

         Continuously improve the digital workplace experience to enable user collaboration and access to any data, anytime, anywhere
         Champion industry-leading cybersecurity and IT lifecycle practices to rapidly design, deliver and sustain world-class mission solutions
         Empower the data workforce, software developers, and application owners through a robust and effective IT platform portfolio
         Modernize IT infrastructure to create lean and diverse transport that brings the power of cloud to the point of mission

Within each of the NGEN performance categories, current and future spending priorities are identified by one of more of the 4 capability-based goals, which define four types of
solutions: Digital Workplace, Cybersecurity & IT Lifecycle, IT Platform, and IT Infrastructure. Additionally, each solution includes capabilities across 14 product groups, such as
Servers, Client Computing, or Threat and Vulnerability Management. The purpose of this reorganization is to align with emerging technology and cyber trends and to improve
consistency and transparency of capability cost.

         Next Generation Enterprise Network Management (NMNM): Funded to deliver offerings across three solution types: Cybersecurity and IT Lifecycle, Digital Workplace,
         and IT Infrastructure to include:

                  IT Operation that supports the delivery of fully integrated, interoperable, and secure networking platform for both CONUS & OCONUS.

                  Develops and maintains business processes and incorporates innovative tools and applications in order to support the transformation into a digital business and a
                  service delivery environment critical to maintaining a competitive advantage against arising global threats.

                  Provides service management of IaaS usage and network utilization. Obtains end user computing devices consisting of physical and virtual device endpoints that
                  allows administrators and end users to access digital data, voice, and video services. Delivers enterprise messaging, ITSM, systems engineering, test and
                  installation services, data center operations, and enterprise tools management and training. Manages network services by controlling and reducing network
                  traffic, internet bandwidth in order to reduce congestion latency and packet loss, and determine causes of network congestion. Acquires warranties and
                  engineering efforts towards enterprise mobility expansion, manages legacy network consolidation/above network base operational requirements, and integrates
                  enterprise service tools that facilitate the full-service lifecycle management of IT customer service requests.

                  Supplies a robust service desk that allows a single point of contact for all end users who require assistance in resolving service disruptions, disseminates
                  communication of changes, maintenance windows, emergencies and other various events affecting the tenant consumed services. Service desk is the focal point
                  for troubleshooting user concerns and provides remediation actions to the engineering team for correction.

                  Integrates tools for Application Performance Monitoring for all services and systems to include: desktop systems (physical and virtual), server systems (physical
                  and virtual) encompassing on-premises legacy environment and IaaS and PaaS environments, and network services to provide monitoring and management for
                  availability and performance to detect and diagnose potential problems in order to maintain the expected level of service for the user and environment. This is
                  essential for ensuring customer satisfaction and system performance validation and will aid in the mission goals of the service desk and full end-to-end support
                  for root cause analysis.

Next Generation Enterprise Network Enterprise Software Management (NMSM): Funded to deliver offerings within Digital Workplace solution type to include:

Management of IT core build and cloud software products and licensing agreements that are contractually negotiated in order to grant the end user the use and rights of
the product needed to operate and execute the mission both ashore and afloat.

        NGEN acquires cloud-based Microsoft 365 (M365) subscription/license to provide Office and other productivity apps, cloud services, device management, and
        Navy security baseline with InTune a cloud based Enterprise Mobility Management (EMM) solution that replaces the legacy Blackberry system. The system will
        also be integrated with the on-prem Microsoft Endpoint Configuration Manager (MECM) to allow enhanced management for all NMCI endpoints including non-
        Government Furnished Equipment (GFE) assets utilizing the current DoN ESL program to the maximum extent. The ESL software agreements enables COTS
        SW licensing by the Government. NGEN will utilize the DoN ESL team, EITSS program office, as the DoN Software Strategic Sourcing office to meet NGEN-R
        requirements through the DoN ESL Program. The DoN software requirements may be satisfied with open-source products software available for free from other
        government contracts, licenses provided to the NGEN-R contractors as GFE, and SW provided to the DoN as a part of a service or supply delivered by the
        NGEN-R contractors. Microsoft licensing assumes a new cost structure based on a personas-based pricing structure.

        Pre-loaded core build software products on all CONUS & OCONUS devices to include:
                Axway Desktop Validator: Used in the validation of digital certificates, including certificates stored on Common Access Cards (CAC) and Personal
                Identity Verification (PIV) cards and in software.
                WinZip: Used as a file archiver and compressor for all window devices.
                ActivID Activclient (NIPR): Used within the NIPR environment to authenticate users when they access the network.
                90Meter (SIPR): Used within the SIPR environment to authenticate users when they access the network.
                Quest Change Auditor: Used to get complete, real-time IT auditing, in-depth forensics and security threat monitoring on all key configuration, user and
                administrator changes.
                Micro Focus Content Manager: Used as the DoN identified source for electronic document and records management system.
                Micro Focus Universal Discovery: Used to collect (by discovery or integrations), reconcile, manage, and present configuration data for hardware,
                software, applications, services, and their interdependencies across on-premises and multi-cloud IT environments.
                Pulse Secure: Used as a Virtual Private Network (VPN) solution for remote and mobile users from any web-enabled device to access network profile.
                Terrago Toolbar: Used as a tool to access, update and share Georeferenced Portable Document Format GeoPDF maps and imagery.
                McAfee Native Encryption Management: Used to protect critical data by managing the native encryption features on all Windows systems.
                Remedy: A client-server trouble ticketing software which is used to track internal problems and customer-reported issues.
                Kelverion: Software that integrates over 300 enterprise IT management tools to enable the automation of Service Requests from the Service Desk.
                Bluecoat: A secure Web gateway (SWG) which is equipped with web filtering, data loss prevention, data inspection, and real-time defense through the
                vendor's global threat network.
                Commvault: Data and information management software focused data protection, data security, compliance and governance, and data transformation.
                Thales Security: A suite of tools that provides advanced data security across Navy devices the IT environment.
                SolarWinds: An IT infrastructure monitoring and management platform which provides centralized monitoring across the Navy's entire IT stack.

Next Generation Enterprise Network Cybersecurity (NMCS): Funded to deliver offerings within Cybersecurity & IT Lifecycle solution type to include:

Employment of the full litany of technical cyber security controls both CONUS & OCONUS that ensure adherence to DoD mandated security controls for the Naval
Enterprise Network. NetScout, ForeScout, Splunk & Verodin consists of a comprehensive cybersecurity framework of tools which provide protections for advanced
endpoint asset management, enhanced network access control, malware detection, and the ability to respond to threats in near real-time that are used to support endpoint
configuration baselines and compliance across thousands of endpoints. Comply to Connect (C2C) is a United States DoD security framework providing the highest level
of assurance for authentication, authorization, compliance assessment, and automated remediation of devices connecting to the enterprise network. C2C is to ensure a
baseline security posture of Department of Defense Information Network (DODIN) endpoints through enforcement of compliance as a condition of Network Access
Control (NAC) leveraging configuration data from the endpoint itself. The C2C capability focuses on enabling discovery, identification, characterization, and reporting
for endpoints attempting to connect to the DODIN. C2C is comprised of:

        NetScout is a system for supporting unified network management across geographic regions and diverse transports (e.g., Wide Area Networks (WAN), Local
        Area Networks (LAN), and peer networks). NetScout system components include real-time dashboards, servers, deployment agents, taps, data collectors, and
        analytics engines. Netscout facilitates real-time enterprise visibility into networks, applications and services. It also inspects traffic (deep packet inspection),
        captures data and forensics, supports data analytics, anomaly detection and performance discovery. Netscout also supports troubleshooting and predictions of
        network performance.

        Forescout hardware and software provides enhanced network access controls, providing authentication for the network and orchestrating the NGEN security
        component to support the Department of Defense (DoD) Chief Information Officer (CIO) Pathfinder Comply-to-Connect (C2C) effort. The Forescout solution
        provides network operators with a capability for scanning, quarantining, and remediation of endpoints that are out of compliance before allowing connection to
        the network.

        Splunk provides the ability to perform investigations based on log records and provides U.S. Fleet Cyber Command/Commander, U.S. Tenth Fleet, Naval
        Network Warfare Command and Navy Cyber Defense Operations Command with real-time Situational Awareness (SA) on network activity, events, status, and
        availability. The solution supports the DoD CIO Pathfinder C2C effort and provides compliance with Information Operations Condition (INFOCON) status of
        information systems which is a method used by the military to defend against a computer network attack.

        Verodin hardware, software, and warranty to deliver the DoD CIO Pathfinder C2C and SA effort. Verodin is used to validate security policies and configurations.
        Results are used to inform network maneuvers to increase security as well as confirm desired effect of such network maneuvers

Next Generation Enterprise Network Information Transport (NMIT): Funded to deliver offerings within IT Infrastructure solution type to include:

Eradicates technical debt for unclassified transport boundaries that will increase port connectivity for users from 100 Megabytes (M) to 1Gigabyte (G) user ports, and
from 1G to 10G bandwidth to Data Centers/Transport Boundaries.

Next Generation Enterprise Network Non-IT Infrastructure (NMNI): Funded to deliver offerings within IT Infrastructure solution type to include:

Capital equipment that directly supports NGEN's IT components and infrastructure throughout CONUS. This property is owned by the Navy, just as the NGEN network
is, and includes systems such as: data center Universal Power Supplies UPSs and batteries, rack-mounted UPSs and batteries, emergency power generating equipment
including fossil fuel tanks, and HVAC systems.

         Uninterruptible Power Supplies (UPS) - There are thousands of rack-mounted UPSs and batteries in the NGEN enterprise which provide back-up, conditioned
         power to the network transport sector equipment housed all over the United States. Reliable functional backup power is critical to providing uninterrupted
         services to users. Due to the age of the UPS units, some material is no longer in production to support repair, so replacement is necessary at several sites across
         the enterprise. Replacement of the UPS Capacitor, HVAC Unit, and fan replacement necessary to ensure the smooth and reliable operation of the UPS. This
         equipment is critical to avoid loss of power to IT equipment throughout the enterprise.

         Emergency Power Generation - Major NGEN sites are those which have data centers and/or server farms. Such sites require back-up power capability for
         potentially longer periods of time than the temporary power UPS units can support. Diesel generators and fuel tanks are located at many of these sites to ensure
         NGEN operations are not interrupted in the event of a long-term power interruption. Many of these generators are past their service life and the fuel tanks
         connected to them are in disrepair.

         HVAC systems - Wherever NGEN gear is in operation, heat management is critical. Temperatures at server farms and data centers must be kept at constant
         levels to avoid the risks associated with IT equipment shutdown because of excessive temperatures.

Next Generation Enterprise Network Cloud Services (NMCL): Funded to deliver offerings across three solution types: Cybersecurity and IT Lifecycle, Digital Workplace,
and IT Platform to include:

Cloud-based software-as-as-service (SaaS) offering that replaces on-premise e-mail infrastructure and additionally offers capabilities like Enterprise Messaging Services,
Collaboration Services, Content & Knowledge management Services, Storage Services, Analytical Services and Cloud-based Telephony services.

         The Navy has migrated to Microsoft MS 365 (M365) suite of tools to enable productivity services for mobile and teleworking users across the network. M365
         suite of services will enhance the Navy's cyber security posture by maturing its Zero Trust principal adoption. The tools required for command control of the IL5
         tenant include (Microsoft Defender for End point, Microsoft Defender for Identity, Microsoft Defender for cloud, Sentinel etc. Engineering and Support Services
         are required to architect, design, implement and operate advanced security, automation and collaboration features needed to provide more flexible access to
         Microsoft. M365 is Microsoft's cloud-based software-as-as-service (SaaS) offering that replaces on premise e-mail infrastructure and additionally offers a suite of
         capabilities like Exchange on line (email), One Drive (for storage), Teams (which includes collaboration, Audio Conferencing and integration with Telephony,

                  Azure to on-board Navy Mission Applications to be hosted on IL5 Azure IAAS (Infrastructure as Service) and M365 while users are operating both off and on
                  the DoD networks. Zero Trust configuration will allow users to have a similar O365 productivity service experience from either the workplace or off-
